candidateFor: Adults with acne scarring, post-acne marks, or ice-pick scars on the face
People bothered by fine lines, wrinkles, or enlarged pores they want to reduce through collagen remodelling
Patients with uneven skin texture, sun damage, or pigmentation including melasma and freckles
Those with lighter skin tones considering ablative CO2 resurfacing, or darker skin tones suited to gentler fractional or laser-toning approaches with conservative settings
Patients able to commit to strict broad-spectrum sun protection and aftercare throughout recovery
People with realistic expectations who understand improvement builds gradually and multiple sessions are often needed

whatToExpect.detail: A dermatologist examines your skin, classifies your skin type, and reviews your medical history including prior treatments and any isotretinoin use. They select the appropriate laser platform and settings for your goals. Many Korean clinics offer remote pre-consultation for international patients so a provisional plan is in place before arrival.

Topical numbing cream is applied for 30–60 minutes beforehand. The dermatologist passes the laser handpiece over target areas; fractional devices create microscopic treated columns while leaving surrounding skin intact to speed healing. You may feel heat or prickling. Soothing serums, a cooling mask, and aftercare products are applied immediately after.

whatToExpect.duration: 1–14 days visible downtime depending on depth; months for full collagen remodelling
whatToExpect.detail: Gentler toning sessions may show only 1–2 days of redness. Fractional CO2 resurfacing typically produces 5–7 days of peeling and redness, with the surface largely healed by days 8–14. Strict broad-spectrum sun protection is required throughout. Most programs stage several sessions a few weeks apart to build results safely.

whyKorea: Seoul's Gangnam district concentrates more specialist dermatology clinics per area than most cities, with practitioners experienced across a wide range of laser platforms rather than relying on a single device for all cases.

Korea's K-beauty culture places high cultural value on skin quality, driving clinical investment in advanced dermatology technology and practitioner expertise with diverse skin types including darker tones.

Medical devices and clinics are overseen by the Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS), with the Korea Health Industry Development Institute (KHIDI) supporting international patient access and coordination.
Per-session costs in Korea generally run approximately $200–$800 for fractional resurfacing, compared with $800–$2,500 or more for equivalent procedures in the United States or Australia.

faqs.question: Which types of laser resurfacing are available in Korea?
faqs.answer: Korean dermatology clinics typically offer fractional CO2 lasers for deeper resurfacing of scars and wrinkles, erbium lasers for gentler superficial treatment, and non-ablative or pigment-focused systems including picosecond and laser-toning devices for melasma, freckles, and dullness. The right device is matched to your skin type and goals during the consultation.

faqs.question: Is laser resurfacing safe for darker skin tones?
faqs.answer: It can be, but deeper skin tones carry a higher risk of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation. Treatment for these tones typically means conservative settings, fractional rather than fully ablative lasers, possible pre-treatment skin conditioning, and strict sun protection afterward. Choose a clinic experienced with diverse skin types and discuss your full skin history at the consultation.

faqs.question: How much does laser skin resurfacing cost in Korea?
faqs.answer: Fractional resurfacing sessions commonly run approximately $200–$800 per session, while fuller ablative treatments can reach roughly $300–$1,500 depending on device, area, and clinic. Gangnam clinics tend to price toward the higher end. Because multiple sessions are usually needed, request a full program estimate and itemised quote from the clinic before committing.

faqs.question: How long is recovery and how long should I stay in Korea?
faqs.answer: Gentler toning sessions may have little visible downtime. Fractional CO2 resurfacing typically involves 5–7 days of redness and peeling, with the surface largely healed by days 8–14. Erbium treatments often heal somewhat faster. Plan your Korea stay so the most visible recovery phase passes before you travel home, and maintain strict sun protection throughout.

faqs.question: How many sessions will I need?
faqs.answer: Most resurfacing plans involve multiple sessions spaced a few weeks apart, since collagen remodelling continues for months after each treatment and results build over time. The number depends on your specific concern, the laser chosen, and how your skin responds. Your dermatologist sets the recommended program during the initial consultation.
